hero.title: Real Hospitality Results From The Anchor
hero.subtitle: Measured outcomes from practical, action-first changes. No vanity metrics, just numbers that matter.

caseStudies.title: Food Costs Eating Your Profits?
caseStudies.subtitle: "I'm working harder but the margins aren't there"
problem: Previous tenants buying food from supermarkets for incredibly low GP
Outdated menu with boring descriptions that didn't sell
Pricing made no sense — no strategy behind it
Food was more of a loss-leader than a profit centre
failed: Tried raising prices without improving quality — customers noticed
Attempted to cut costs with cheaper ingredients — quality suffered
solution: Partnered with commercial suppliers (Barrel And Stone, Brakes, Bidfood)
Used AI to analyse menu psychology and rewrite descriptions using sensory language
Implemented strategic pricing and menu positioning
Introduced stone-baked pizzas as high-margin hero product
Regular AI-powered menu updates based on seasonal ingredients and costs
learnings: Quality improvements justify price increases
Sensory menu language lifts high-margin choices
High-margin hero products can transform food sales
Consistent supplier relationships improve both cost and quality
caseStudies.quote: We went from treating food as a necessary evil to making it a real profit driver. The menu rewrite alone shifted what people were ordering.

caseStudies.title: Dead Midweek Nights?
caseStudies.subtitle: "Tuesday to Thursday barely covers the staff costs"
problem: Existing quiz had inconsistent turnout
Questions were too hard or too obscure
No social media promotion or consistent visibility
Limited prizes and incentives to keep people coming back
failed: Making questions easier — lost the competitive edge
Changing the day — confused regular customers
Offering bigger prizes — ate into profits without improving numbers
solution: AI-generated balanced question sets across different knowledge levels
Automated social media promotion with engaging quiz teasers
Introduced team-building elements and friendly rivalry
Created loyalty system with points and seasonal championships
Optimised timing and format based on customer feedback

results.value: Teams booking tables weeks in advance

learnings: Balance is key — questions need variety in difficulty
Social media teasers build anticipation and attendance
Team dynamics matter more than individual competition
Consistency in quality keeps people coming back
caseStudies.quote: Our quiz night went from 'something we did' to 'the highlight of the week'. The community it built is worth more than the direct revenue.

caseStudies.title: Invisible Online?
caseStudies.subtitle: "We post but nobody sees it, nobody comes"
problem: Spending hours weekly creating social media content
Inconsistent posting leading to algorithm penalties
Low response and reach despite the effort
Running out of content ideas constantly
failed: Batch creating content manually — still took too long
Hiring a social media manager — didn't understand pub life
Using generic scheduling tools — content felt robotic
solution: AI-powered content creation system generating 30 days of posts in 2 hours
Automated posting across Facebook, Instagram, Twitter
Local SEO optimisation for pub-specific searches
Performance tracking and AI-driven optimisation
Custom templates maintaining brand voice and personality
timeInvestment: Initial setup plus 2 hours monthly batching
learnings: Consistency trumps perfection in social media
AI can maintain brand voice when properly trained
Local SEO integration multiplies social media impact
Automation frees time for actual customer interaction
caseStudies.quote: Social media went from a chore to our best marketing tool. We're reaching people we never would have found — and they're walking through the door.

caseStudies.title: Sunday Service Out of Control?
caseStudies.subtitle: "I'm throwing food away every week and it's killing me"
problem: Huge food waste from unpredictable Sunday lunch numbers
Running out of popular dishes while having surplus of others
Customer disappointment when favourite dishes unavailable
Staff stress from not knowing how much to prepare
failed: Guessing based on weather forecasts — still inaccurate
Reducing menu options — lost customer variety
Cook-to-order system — too slow for Sunday volume
solution: Created easy online preorder system with payment processing
Automated reminder system for regular customers
Integration with kitchen prep schedules so the team knows exactly what to cook
Small deposit system to reduce no-shows
learnings: Customers appreciate knowing their meal is guaranteed
Preorders create commitment — lower no-show rates
Predictable demand enables better ingredient purchasing
Staff performance improves with clear preparation targets
caseStudies.quote: Sunday lunch went from our most stressful service to our most profitable. Knowing exactly what to cook changed everything.

caseStudies.title: Can't Compete With Chains?
caseStudies.subtitle: "They've got the budget, I've got nothing"
problem: Quiet Wednesday nights with almost no revenue
Customers viewing the pub as 'just a local'
No differentiation from chain pub competition
Untapped potential for premium offerings
failed: Generic wine tastings — not engaging enough
Partnering with random suppliers — no cohesive experience
Pricing too low — attracted the wrong crowd
solution: Curated premium experiences with storytelling elements
Partnerships with quality local suppliers and artisans
Educational component — customers learn while tasting
Proper pricing reflecting the premium experience
Limited spaces creating exclusivity and demand
learnings: Premium pricing attracts customers who value quality
Education transforms tasting from consumption to experience
Scarcity creates demand — limited spaces increase bookings
Personal storytelling connects customers to the experience
caseStudies.quote: These tasting nights transformed how people see us. We're not just the local anymore — we're a destination.
